phase-scope.phase-27: Email delivery foundation only: fake-provider tests must prove request shaping, template rendering, provider error mapping, and redaction before live provider use.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization regression gate proves verification-email delivery still participates in pending registration without leaking tokens or provider secrets.
phase-scope.phase-36: Resend production wiring proves clean deployments enable EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend without exposing API keys or verification tokens.

verification-levels.module-local: Unit tests use a fake provider/client and never require live email credentials.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-27 combines this suite with V-M-041 guard tests and MyGRACE XML lint.
verification-levels.integration-later: Live provider smoke is operator-run only after environment secrets are configured outside tests.

module-checks.check-1: Email template renders readable UTF-8 subject, HTML, and text fallback with KrotPN branding.
module-checks.check-2: Verification link/code is accepted as input but never appears in logs, exceptions, or provider error payloads.
module-checks.check-3: Provider success returns a typed delivery receipt containing only safe message metadata.
module-checks.check-4: Provider 401, 429, 5xx, timeout, and transport failures map to stable internal error codes.
module-checks.check-5: Missing or disabled provider configuration blocks send with an operator-safe error and no network call.
module-checks.check-6: Phase-34 full registration suite confirms email delivery is invoked only for pending registration and never after MTProto owner payload generation.
module-checks.check-7: Resend request shape uses https://api.resend.com/emails, Authorization Bearer API key, noreply@krotpn.xyz sender, and HTML/text verification template payload.
module-checks.check-8: Deploy smoke proves EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and RESEND_API_KEY are present when operator supplied the key, while logs and tests redact the key.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verification email is sent to a normalized allowed email address through the fake provider.
scenarios.scenario-2: Provider authentication, rate-limit, server, timeout, and transport errors are surfaced as safe typed failures.
scenarios.scenario-3: Logs never contain verification token, verification URL, API key, or raw provider response body.
scenarios.scenario-4: Provider-disabled configuration returns a typed disabled-provider result before request construction.
scenarios.scenario-5: Clean VPS deployment with a supplied Resend API key sends pending-registration verification email through Resend.
scenarios.scenario-6: Missing Resend API key fails with an operator-safe configuration error and no live registration side effects.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never log verification links, tokens, provider API keys, SMTP credentials, or full provider response bodies.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Email addresses in logs must be normalized and masked where possible; provider message IDs are allowed.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-27 tests are deterministic and fake-provider-only; no deploy, install, DNS, TLS, or live email provider changes are allowed.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-13: backend/tests/test_email_delivery.py passed locally, covering template rendering, fake-provider dispatch, provider-disabled guard, redaction, and safe error mapping.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: Phase-36 planning scoped Resend deploy wiring after live clean VPS registration returned provider_disabled because EMAIL_PROVIDER was left disabled.
